text:
Once upon a time in a little town called Hawthorne, there lived an unusually polite bluejay named Buzz. Buzz didn't just talk; he could mimic the human languages he heard. One autumn afternoon, the town held a costume contest.

Every child wanted to wear something spooky, but Buzz fancied himself a historical figure. He chose Edward Scissorhands. He spent weeks collecting antique scissors everywhere—scissors for flowers, scissors for knitting... you get the idea.

The big night came, and Buzz flew around town, cutting out little Edward-like figures from all sorts of fabrics. There was Edward playing chess with a flamingo, one holding a butterfly.

When it was his turn to walk the runway, Buzz fluttered down the sidewalk like a bat out of hell. People gasped as Edward floated along calmly, scissors gliding through air.

Afterward, everyone wanted to know Edward’s secret. He just practiced really well every time he accidentally clipped something.

Buzz was acclaimed as the finest Edward
